mutation: goal: replace contour-by-contour torch scheduling with a minimum-cost open-trail decomposition (Eulerization) of a shared-edge cut graph, coupling pierce count to a graph invariant (odd-degree vertices) and airtime to a geometric matching/sequencing choice
theme: One torch, many parts, least dead travel
title: One Torch, Many Parts: Least Dead Travel
strategies: trivial: pierce-cut-retract every required edge individually, in input order (no batching at all)
greedy: one pierce per part, contour its remaining (unclaimed) edges, visit parts in input order -- the universal CAM baseline

strong: Eulerize the cut graph -- pair odd-degree vertices with nearest-neighbor phantom (airtime) edges, take one Eulerian circuit over cuts+phantoms via Hierholzer, and slice it into trails at the phantom crossings, dropping the priciest phantom as the unclosed loop boundary

reference-headroom: exact minimum-weight perfect matching (e.g. Blossom) on odd-degree vertices instead of nearest-neighbor, plus cross-component trail-order optimization, both left as headroom above the reference strong solution
